text:
Once upon a time, there was a young boy named Alex. Alex loved the sea. Every summer, he would row his kayak deep into the heart of the ocean.

After one particularly long rainy season, Alex realized he couldn't return to his beloved sea adventures.

One winter day, snow drifted white against the windowpanes, a stark contrast to the blue sky. Alex rummaged through boxes under his bed, desperately searching for something to keep him sane.

Out came a small box, so old it had no edges. Inside lay a faded photo. It wasn't a beautiful picture; it was just an ordinary photograph of the ocean. But there were rows of tiny shells scattered across the seabirds' nest imprint. Those weren't the shells he found hundreds of times before.

Curious, Alex pressed the lens between his fingers. The photo popped out vividly, breathing ocean life. That's when it hit him. He could still feel the saltwater on his skin.

With tears
